concerns.text: Task T011 (Integration test for dataset content) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly states 'Run AFTER T012/T013 implementation'. This creates a hidden ordering dependency that violates the [P] tag and the 'Producer before Consumer' rule. T011 consumes the artifact (filtered dataset schema) produced by T012/T013, so it cannot run in parallel with them.

Task T034 (Extend glmm.py to calculate 'Cost of Reconfiguration') is placed in Phase 6 but depends on T035 (Implement learning_state.py) and T037 (Modify dual_track.py to log reconfiguration). T034 consumes artifacts (state tracking, log flags) that do not exist until T035 and T037 are complete. The current order implies T034 runs before its data sources are ready.

Task T031 (Compare rule-based detection vs human ground truth) is listed in Phase 5 but depends on T030 (Generate annotation sample PDF) and the actual human annotation process. T031 consumes the 'human-annotated ground truth' which is not produced by T030 (which only generates the sample for review). The task order implies T031 can run immediately after T030, but the required input (annotations) is missing from the task list, creating a logical gap in the data flow.

Phase 6 (Turing Review) tasks (T034-T040) are listed as a separate phase but depend on US3 infrastructure (T027-T033). The dependency section correctly notes this, but the task list places T034 before T035/T037, violating the internal data flow of the 'Learning vs Search' metric calculation. T034 should follow T035 and T037.
